//4: All data in this platform is SYNTHETIC. There is no real PHI/PII.
//PRIMARY2: Cursor can connect to a remote MCP server directly via a URL. Authenticate
//PRIMARY3: as CLAIMS_MCP_READER with a short-lived PAT/OAuth token in the header.

snowflake-claims-managed.//note: PRIMARY. Remote Snowflake-managed MCP endpoint. Native URL transport.

mcpServers.//FALLBACK: FALLBACK ONLY (not primary): local custom stdio server. Use only when
mcpServers.//FALLBACK2: the Snowflake-managed MCP server is unavailable. Read-only, SELECT-only,
mcpServers.//FALLBACK3: schema-allowlisted, logs to AUDIT.MCP_QUERY_LOG. Remove the leading
mcpServers.//FALLBACK4: underscore from the key below to enable.

_snowflake-claims-fallback.//note: FALLBACK ONLY. Local Python stdio server. Prefer the managed server above.

mcpServers.//FALLBACK_SSO: FALLBACK ONLY (local-dev SSO variant): same local stdio server
mcpServers.//FALLBACK_SSO2: with externalbrowser (Snowflake SSO) auth. Opens a browser to log in;
mcpServers.//FALLBACK_SSO3: use only on a machine with a browser. Remove the leading underscore
mcpServers.//FALLBACK_SSO4: from the key below to enable.

_snowflake-claims-fallback-sso.//note: FALLBACK ONLY. Local Python stdio server with externalbrowser (SSO) auth.
